title: Nonatelia tomentosa

This family is part of the order Magnoliales, which is a group of flowering plants characterized by their distinctive flowers and seeds. The order Magnoliales is further grouped within the class Magnoliopsida, which is a subclass of flowering plants known as dicotyledons. These flowering plants are known for having two cotyledons in their seeds and typically having a seed coat that splits open to release the seed. The classification of Nonatelia tomentosa is a part of the larger taxonomic system used in botany.
